denominator_check.sample_caveat: n=15 trades (13–17 Jul). All conclusions are directional only — not statistically reliable.
denominator_check.verdict: PARTLY PERSISTS AFTER CONTROLLING — median MAE in points is similar across SL groups (R-gap is partly a denominator artifact), but VWAP-effective remains worse on MAE/ATR and ATR-normalized single-candle blowouts. Avg MAE points look higher for VWAP but medians are nearly identical (outlier-driven, not typical). Treat as directional only (n=15), not a settled finding.

pre_entry_discrimination.sample_caveat: n=15 trades. Win = P&L points > 0; loss/flat = P&L ≤ 0. Directional only.

combined.note: Flag rate on winners (66.7%) vs losers (77.8%) differs by 11 pp (≤15 pp) — not currently discriminating.

volume_decel.note: Flag rate on winners (0.0%) vs losers (33.3%) differs by 33 pp — higher on losers. Directional only (small n).
volume_decel.label: volume decelerating into entry

body_shrink.note: Flag rate on winners (16.7%) vs losers (22.2%) differs by 6 pp (≤15 pp) — not currently discriminating.
body_shrink.label: candle body shrinking into entry (momentum fade)

ema_vwap_misalign.note: Flag rate on winners (16.7%) vs losers (22.2%) differs by 6 pp (≤15 pp) — not currently discriminating.
ema_vwap_misalign.label: EMA10/VWAP misaligned with trade direction

wide_ema_vwap_gap.note: Flag rate on winners (50.0%) vs losers (22.2%) differs by 28 pp — higher on winners. Directional only (small n).
wide_ema_vwap_gap.label: wide EMA10–VWAP gap pre-entry

ema5_sep_fade.note: Flag rate on winners (16.7%) vs losers (22.2%) differs by 6 pp (≤15 pp) — not currently discriminating.
ema5_sep_fade.label: price losing separation from EMA5 into entry

pre_entry_discrimination.overall_note: Combined PRE_ENTRY_WARNING is not discriminating, but one or more sub-flags show a larger win/loss rate gap — see breakdown. Still directional only (n=15).

summary.sample_caveat: Sample is only 15 trades (13–17 Jul 2026). Findings are directional research signals, not statistically reliable conclusions.
